milestone: Creator Governing Rules Audit — a Confirmed Text-Alignment Fidelity Violation Found and Fixed
slice: Audited the just-shipped Stretch image-fit and Text-newline fixes against CLAUDE.md's own 5 permanent Creator Governing Rules (Fidelity/Guardrails/Refinement/Personalization/Publish Fidelity) per direct instruction: "check for both fixes against the rules which needs to be honored always." Both fixes confirmed fully compliant, with two positive side-effects found: the Story-Author "Words" textarea in js/contextPanel.js's _renderWorldObjectDisclosure automatically benefits from the newline fix (Refinement/Personalization), and js/storyDestinations.js's Publish path already routes through the identical SlideRenderer.buildPayload/render() pair the editor canvas uses, satisfying Publish Fidelity by construction for any renderer.js fix. Auditing surfaced one genuine, separate, previously-disclosed-but-unfixed Rule 1 (Fidelity) violation: convergeSceneLayer (tools/world-builder-v2/js/services/builder.js, kept in lockstep with the identical fix in tools/world-builder/js/services/builder.js) always compiled a Text Experience's anchor as hardcoded 'top-left', silently discarding the Scene Layer's own real, authored align field (left/center/right -- already correctly honoured by engineRuntime.js's own _paintLayer in Builder's own Working View/Runtime Preview) -- so a centred or right-aligned Text Experience rendered correctly in Builder but always came back left-aligned once compiled/Published, a direct violation of "nothing about a Scene's... content is silently discarded or substituted on the way into Creator." Fixed by deriving the compiled anchor's horizontal component from layer.align ('center'->'top-center', 'right'->'top-right', else 'top-left', matching layerEngine.js's own 9-point resolveAnchor vocabulary exactly) in both Builder versions' identical convergeSceneLayer text branch -- vertical positioning stays 'top' always, unchanged, matching both renderers' own existing top-anchored behaviour for text regardless of alignment. Verified via a new 8-assertion Playwright suite: both v1 and v2's real convergeSceneLayer correctly map left/center/right/unset align to top-left/top-center/top-right/top-left; an end-to-end Studio render confirms a compiled anchor:'top-center' entry genuinely sets ctx.textAlign to 'center' while SlideRenderer.render() paints it, not just a compile-step string. Full regression: the newline fix's own 7-assertion suite and the Stretch fit's own 6-assertion suite both re-run unchanged, plus both goldenBuild.js suites pass unchanged. See CLAUDE.md's own entry for the full account.
